title: practical english usage - Part 161
content:
superlative (or first, etc) has a subject relationship with the following verb. In Note that this structure is only possible in cases where the noun with the The next to speak was Mrs Fenshaw.

Who was the first woman to climb Everest?

This structure is also common after first, last and next.

Than often replaces a subject or object pronoun or an adverbial expression, 4 words     left   out   after    than not She’s twice as lively as her sister. (     ... twice livelier...) Note that twice and half are not possible in this structure.

It was ten times more difficult than I expected.

She can walk three times faster than you.

+ comparative.

Instead of three/four, etc times as much  (►  203.7), we  can use three/four, 3 three      times      .. .er,   etc grammar •  208  comparison: advanced points Who can run (the) fastest?

The is sometimes dropped before superlative adverbs in an informal style.

work is. (A woman is being compared with a man - the is possible.) - She works (the) hardest in the family; her husband doesn't know what Section17 Comparison grammar •  Section 18   Prepositions (before) and ►    579 (since).

Some prepositions can also function as conjunctions: ►    249 (after), ►    250 She picked it up. (adverb particle) She ran up the stairs. (preposition) ►     12-13.

particles. For the difference, ►     195. For verbs with prepositions and particles, Words like on, off, up, down can function both as prepositions and as adverb prepositions,         adverb      particles      and   conjunctions The question (of) whether they should turn back was never discussed.

I'm not certain (of) what I'm supposed to do.

For details, >210.

Prepositions are sometimes dropped before conjunctions and sometimes not.

prepositions         before     conjunctions I look forward to seeing you soon.

When  to is a preposition, it is also followed by -ing forms. (►    104.2) She keeps fit by going to the gym.

For details, ►     104, 211.

When we  use verbs after prepositions, we  use -ing forms, not infinitives.

What are you thinking about? She's not very easy to talk to.

especially in an informal style. For details, ►    209.

In English, prepositions can come  at the ends of clauses in certain structures, word    order in another language; in other expressions the opposite is true.

whole.
